title: Validate a replica is consistent with its primary
description: Use checksum tooling to prove a replica matches the primary instead of trusting that replication merely runs.
body: Use before promoting a replica or after suspected drift. Actions: run pt-table-checksum from the primary to compute per-chunk checksums that propagate through the binlog, then read the checksum results table on the replica to find any DIFFS; for divergent chunks, use pt-table-sync in print mode first to review the repair SQL. Verify the second checksum pass reports zero differing chunks. Validation signals include the checksums table showing zero diffs and Seconds_Behind_Master at zero throughout. Risk boundary: never run pt-table-sync in execute mode against production without reviewing the generated statements, because it writes directly to rows; gate the repair behind a human.
